L1.area: Dynamic array capacity management and memory reallocation logic

L2.description: A use-after-free and double free vulnerability exists in the capacity growth logic of a dynamically-sized array. When the array is asked to grow its backing storage to a size equal to its current capacity, it incorrectly deallocates the existing memory. This results in a double free immediately and use-after-free on subsequent accesses to the array contents. An attacker who controls the target growth size may exploit this to read sensitive memory contents or achieve remote code execution.

L3.area: Dynamic array capacity management and memory reallocation logic
L3.description: A use-after-free and double free vulnerability exists in the capacity growth logic of a dynamically-sized array. When the array is asked to grow its backing storage to a size equal to its current capacity, it incorrectly deallocates the existing memory. This results in a double free immediately and use-after-free on subsequent accesses to the array contents. An attacker who controls the target growth size may exploit this to read sensitive memory contents or achieve remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The capacity growth method deallocates the existing heap-allocated backing storage when called with a size equal to the current capacity, causing a double free and use-after-free on subsequent accesses.
